title: How do I start learning Machine Learning in 2026?
content: I know basic Python but I feel overwhelmed by the number of courses available. Should I start with mathematics first or jump directly into machine learning?

title: Comment
content: Start with Andrew Ng's Machine Learning Specialization. Learn NumPy, Pandas and Scikit-Learn, then build 2-3 projects before moving to deep learning.

title: Machine Learning Roadmap
content: A complete roadmap for learning machine learning, covering Python, mathematics, statistics, supervised learning, deep learning, MLOps and project ideas.

title: Awesome Machine Learning
content: Curated list of machine learning frameworks, libraries, tutorials, papers, courses and tools maintained by the community.

title: Random ML Scripts
content: Collection of experimental scripts I wrote while learning Python.

title: A Survey of Large Language Models
content: This paper reviews the architecture, training methods, evaluation benchmarks and applications of modern large language models.

title: Machine Learning for Beginners
content: An educational review explaining supervised learning, unsupervised learning, model evaluation and common beginner mistakes.

title: Is mathematics necessary for machine learning?
content: Many beginners get stuck trying to learn all of linear algebra before starting ML. How much math is actually required?

title: Comment
content: Learn the basics of linear algebra and probability as you go. Don't spend six months studying math before touching any ML project.
